title: Science, Technology

vocabulary.meaning: an important new discovery or invention in technology
vocabulary.example: Artificial intelligence may become the next major technological breakthrough.

vocabulary.meaning: to make daily life easier, healthier, or more comfortable
vocabulary.example: Medical technology can improve quality of life for many patients.

vocabulary.meaning: worries about whether something is morally right or wrong
vocabulary.example: Gene editing raises serious ethical concerns.

vocabulary.meaning: results that were not planned or expected
vocabulary.example: New inventions sometimes create unintended consequences for society.

vocabulary.meaning: to control how new technology is developed or used
vocabulary.example: Governments need to regulate innovation without stopping progress.

vocabulary.meaning: to benefit society as a whole
vocabulary.example: Scientific research should serve the public good, not only private profit.

questions: What are some good things that science has done?
What are some bad things that science has done?
Which technological things do you like the most and why?
Which technological things do you like the least and why?
Which technological item do you think has benefited mankind the most? Why?
Scientific measures should be standardized throughout the world. We should all use the metric system all of the time. Do you agree?
Science is more important than religion in today's world. Do you agree?
What do you think are the main dangers of scientific advances? What laws do you think we need to protect societies from these dangers?
Are most scientists in your country working on defence assignments or on non-military projects?
